articles.title: IN THE AGE OF AI, WEBSITES WILL BE TRANSFORMED
articles.summary: Websites will move from static pages to agent-powered interfaces. Traditional navigation will give way to intelligent, interactive experiences. Users will interact with AI agents that understand their needs and curate content in real time. Layouts will adapt dynamically, creating personalized, conversational web experiences. This shift could change how information is accessed and how sites are structured. Even AI-driven traffic may affect how platforms handle visitors.

articles.title: THE HIDDEN COST OF VIEWBOTTING
articles.summary: Viewbotting is undermining trust in live streaming by inflating metrics and wasting marketing budgets. Twitch logged 30M and Kick nearly 20M hours of fake watch time in Q2 2025. To combat it, brands should focus on verified engagement over view counts, monitor behavioral red flags like unusually stable audiences or low chat activity, and use advanced analytics and third-party verification.

articles.title: REDDIT IS WINNING THE BATTLE FOR TRANSACTIONAL KEYWORDS
articles.summary: Reddit now drives more bottom-of-funnel traffic than G2 or Capterra, as Google rewards authentic discussions over polished SEO content. Brands that invest in review platforms are missing where real buying decisions happen. To compete, they should build a presence using a subreddit, a brand-owned account, and a user persona for organic participation. Tactics include joining high-intent threads and starting buyer-led discussions. This builds SEO visibility and can influence AI-generated search results.

articles.title: DOES THE WORLD NEED ANOTHER COOKBOOK? YES
articles.summary: Cook's Illustrated engages readers by using images to show key points and product benefits. It guides different audience segments to what interests them. The content surprises readers with unexpected insights, giving them something to share. It creates a sense of depth and value, offering useful information before asking for a purchase. Visual demonstration, clear guidance, moments of surprise, and a perception of depth lead readers to act and build trust.

articles.title: HOWIE
articles.summary: Howie is an email-based scheduling assistant that manages calendars like an EA. It handles meeting coordination, rescheduling, conflict resolution, recurring 1:1s, and follow-ups. It integrates with Google Calendar and Zoom.

articles.title: TOO MUCH FEEDBACK, TOO LITTLE CLARITY? HERE'S HOW PRODUCTBOARD'S AI FIXED IT
articles.summary: Productboard's AI-powered solution, Pulse, helps brands turn disorganized customer feedback into actionable insights. Arena by PTC used Pulse to analyze thousands of customer inputs instantly, which ensured decisions were based on real demand instead of assumptions. The platform automates feedback categorization and connects insights directly to product roadmaps, creating a single source of truth across teams.

articles.title: DOES SIMPLE CONVERT BETTER? 4 “BORING” SALES FUNNELS ANALYZED
articles.summary: Complex funnels underperform when design outweighs clarity. Grasshopper used a simple path from homepage to pricing to checkout. KlientBoost centered on a valuable offer with a short form to reduce cognitive load. Growbo educated prospects with blog content, offered a lead magnet, and ended with a concise pricing page. All focused on sequencing and delivering real value rather than flashy design or elaborate UX.

articles.title: THREADS MARKETING MISTAKES THAT COST ITS GROWTH
articles.summary: Threads gained 100M sign-ups in five days, but poor positioning and missing features like search and hashtags caused engagement to collapse. Overactive moderation, misaligned algorithms, and a lack of marketing tools further eroded trust. Meta has since added features and stabilized growth, but Threads' early mistakes show that hype without clarity leads to rapid decline.

articles.title: GOOGLE ADS NOW FLAGS LOW-QUALITY IMAGES
articles.summary: Google Ads now provides AI-driven suggestions to improve image quality directly in the Recommendations tab.

articles.title: OPENAI'S MARKETING EFFORTS ARE EMBARRASSINGLY INEFFECTIVE
articles.summary: OpenAI's new ChatGPT ads look good but fail to connect with humans.

articles.title: CMOS SHARE WHAT BRANDS GET WRONG WHEN MARKETING TO GEN Z
articles.summary: Gen Z doesn't hate advertising but ignores anything that feels out of touch.
